# Staged Autonomy

## Overview

A pattern where an LLM earns autonomy over time by first operating under
human supervision. The agent demonstrates it has internalized the
domain's schema before being trusted to work unsupervised.

## Key Claims

- Early in a new domain, LLMs produce messy ontology if unsupervised
- Human-in-loop for the first N sources establishes patterns
- Once patterns stabilize, the LLM earns more autonomy

## Counter-arguments

*None identified yet.*

## Data Gaps

*None identified yet — what's the right threshold for N?*
